cards.back: A concept in game theory where the optimal strategy for each player in a sequence of sub-games is independent of the other sub-games, and the overall equilibrium is achieved when no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy
A concept in game theory where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged
A smaller game within a larger game, where the outcome of the sub-game affects the strategy of the players in the larger game
A concept in game theory where the optimal strategy for each player is chosen, assuming all players know each other's strategies and the game is repeated infinitely
A concept in game theory where the optimal strategy for each player is chosen, assuming the players make their decisions one after the other
